planned_changes.description: Add a new subsection in commissioning_note.md titled 'Priority weight rationale' that (a) states the default weights used, (b) provides a concise engineering justification for equal weights (or cites a packet-standard), and (c) references sensitivity results demonstrating that small weight perturbations do not change the recommended remediation.

Document the origin/rationale for the droop_pass < 0.2 rule in commissioning_note.md and add a cell-level comment or cell reference in the workbook (e.g., Packet_Assumptions!A#, Record_Assessment![cell]) linking the rule to its justification. If no authoritative source exists, mark the rule 'needs_review' and add an explicit reviewer action item.

Produce CSV exports for the five key sheets (Packet_Assumptions.csv, Record_Assessment.csv, Remediation_and_Decision.csv, Sensitivity.csv, Final_Decision.csv) and place them in attempts/baseline/artifacts/ alongside the .xlsx. Alternatively, rebuild the .xlsx using openpyxl/xlsxwriter if available so it is programmatically openable. Add a short note in commissioning_note.md stating which option was used.

In the commissioning_note.md final decision table, include explicit numeric baseline and post-action risk values and exact spreadsheet cell references used to compute them (e.g., 'Baseline max risk = 82.1 (Record_Assessment!B10); Post-action max risk (C1+2) = 57.5 (Remediation_and_Decision!I6); reduction = 29.9%').

If manual OOXML generation is retained, add a brief README (artifact reader_notes.txt) describing the OOXML assembly approach, how to map sheets/cells to packet rows, and listing the included CSV exports to facilitate automated checks.

raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Recover semantic credit by adding engineering justification for default weights and a traceable justification for the droop_pass cutoff; remove automated-parsing friction so graders can verify formulas and cell-level citations.

raw_model_output.revision_reason: Recover semantic credit by adding engineering justification for default weights and a traceable justification for the droop_pass cutoff; remove automated-parsing friction so graders can verify formulas and cell-level citations.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Good deliverables: both required artifacts are present, executable logic documented, remediation combinations enumerated with correct cost arithmetic, and a sensitivity pass implemented. Primary gaps preventing full semantic credit are (1) lack of an engineering justification or trace for the default priority weights (severity/recency/exposure set to 1) and (2) the droop_pass cutoff (<0.2) is asserted as a hard rule without tracing or rationale. Additionally, the spreadsheet cannot be programmatically parsed in the grader environment (openpyxl unavailable), which increases verification friction. Addressing these will recover full credit and improve automated verifiability.
